note: Manual layer of dashboard/portfolio-hq.html: everything here comes from live PostHog reads, founder statements, or weekly readouts — not derivable from status.json. Update this file (and bump asOf), then run ./agentic-os refresh — it regenerates portfolio-hq.html as its last step.
verifiedNote: Per-block last-verified dates. asOf is when this file was last written; these are when each block's claims were last checked against a real source (git, App Store, PostHog). The refresh flags any block older than 7 days as a cross-layer issue, so a stale block cannot hide under a fresh asOf. Bump a date ONLY after re-verifying that block against its source.

founderCommand.title: BOTH APPS LIVE — and the sign-in P0 is REFUTED, not fixed. Read this before acting on anything else
founderCommand.body: Store-verified via Apple's lookup API 2026-07-22, not founder report: RunSmart 1.1.2 (27) live 17:04:47Z (id6768297840), Resumely 1.4.5 (15) live 15:23:44Z (id6776752349). Neither is a feature release. THE CORRECTION THAT OUTRANKS BOTH: the "Sign in with Apple is broken for every new user" P0 is refuted. The revoke-and-retry test (Stop Using Apple ID -> delete -> reinstall public 1.1.1) completed a genuine first-time consent flow: sign_in_completed 10:34:21Z, zero sign_in_failed, new auth.identities row 10:34:20Z. Two reasoning errors caused the wrong verdict and both generalize: (1) a zero-success window at ~26 launchers/month cannot distinguish "broken for everyone" from "broken for some" — the p~0.007 figure assumed the uniformity it was meant to test; (2) Apple's sub is stable across revoke, so a new identity row proves "new to Supabase", never "new to Apple". Also iPhone99,7 is not a real Apple model, so 3 real devices failed, not 4. STILL UNEXPLAINED AND STILL SERIOUS: 10 code-1000 events across 3 real devices in July with 0 completions on any, and 0 non-founder signups since 2026-06-18. THE TRAP: this reads as good news and invites relief-driven inaction. "Not broken for everyone" is not "not broken". The front door still fails for an unknown fraction of real users and nobody knows why.
founderCommand.prompt: No session. Read-only correction card. Do not open WP-53 on this — see the WP-53 hold card.

founderCommand.title: #1 BUILD — Resumely has NO review prompt and ZERO ratings. This is the only lever that compounds while the activation counter fills
founderCommand.body: Verified via Apple lookup API today: both apps read averageUserRating 0, userRatingCount 0. Resumely is the primary product at ~55 users/7d and has been live since June, and the ONLY requestReview in its entire codebase is a `// TODO:` comment at ResumeBuilder IOS APP/Ambassador/AmbassadorSuccessView.swift:37 — there is no prompt on the main success path at all. RunSmart has a real AppStoreReviewPrompt.swift, but it fires from requestAfterFirstRunIfNeeded() and zero organic users have ever completed a run, so it has effectively never fired either. Why this is #1 and not housekeeping: the EXD-022 count gate needs volume, ratings are the highest-leverage free conversion lever on the App Store, and this is the one piece of product work that pays off during the 14 days you are otherwise just waiting for a cohort. Ship it in 1.4.6 on the export-success path, gated so it cannot fire before a real export.
founderCommand.prompt:
Resumely iOS. Add a StoreKit review request on the genuine success moment and ship it as 1.4.6 (16).

Context: the app has zero App Store ratings and no review prompt on the main path — the only requestReview reference is a `// TODO:` at Ambassador/AmbassadorSuccessView.swift:37.

Requirements:
1. Trigger on export success only, after the PDF is actually delivered — never on app launch, never on optimization completion, never on a failure path.
2. Gate it: fire at most once per app version, and only for a person who has completed at least one export. Persist the gate so a reinstall does not immediately re-ask.
3. Do NOT fire for is_internal_tester persons or in Debug builds — WP-48 S2-B is still open and internal traffic is 27% of persons.
4. Use SKStoreReviewController / RequestReviewAction via the SwiftUI environment; respect that iOS may silently decline to show it, so never block UI or assume it appeared.
5. Emit an analytics event when the request is issued so the ask rate is measurable — this is the point.
6. Red-first tests: gate logic (once per version, export required, internal excluded) before implementation.
7. Resolve or delete the dead TODO at AmbassadorSuccessView.swift:37 so there is one code path, not two.

Then bump to 1.4.6 (16), run the full suite plus EN and HE launch smoke on iOS 26.5 (NOT 26.3.1 — it crashes the XCTest host), and stop at the founder-controlled archive boundary. Do not touch the WP-51 milestone or the WP-50 denominator.

founderCommand.title: #2 FOUNDER, TOMORROW (2026-07-23) — 10-minute T+1 telemetry check on both apps
founderCommand.body: The cheapest possible check and it gates every later read: do events arrive carrying app_version 1.1.2 and app_version 1.4.5? If yes, both builds are reaching real installs and attribution survived the fresh-install path — which also re-validates the 1.1.1 fix for analytics losing app version/build after a clean install. If no events carry the new versions after 24h, stop and diagnose that before trusting anything at T+7. Read hygiene applies even here: exclude the founder's own person and is_internal_tester, and fingerprint the PostHog project first — the MCP banner has claimed project 171597 while serving 270848.
founderCommand.prompt: PostHog check, both projects. Fingerprint each project first (365d event/people counts) to confirm which one you are actually served, then for the last 24h: does any non-founder, non-is_internal_tester event carry app_version = 1.1.2 (project 171597, RunSmart) and app_version = 1.4.5 (project 270848, Resumely)? Report raw counts and the person count, not a rate. This is a binary reachability check, not an activation read — do not compute a funnel from it.

founderCommand.title: #3 FOUNDER, ~20 MINUTES, NO BUILD REQUIRED — two ASO drafts written 11 days ago and never submitted
founderCommand.body: Promotional text can be changed in App Store Connect without shipping a build or triggering review, so this is pure unclaimed upside sitting in two repos. RunSmart: rs-aso-002-draft in .agent-os/distribution/messaging.md, sourced (Relph et al. 2023 on the 27.3% Couch-to-5K completion rate, and the corrected Frandsen et al. BJSM 2025 mechanism replacing the sloppy '10% rule' claim). Resumely: the anti-generic-AI promo variant in docs/app-store/en-metadata.md, sourced from TeamBlind evidence that AI resumes read as obviously ChatGPT-written. Both were drafted 2026-07-11 and neither has ever been submitted. Caveat carried forward honestly: Runna already ships a named 'Post-Injury Plan', so RunSmart's returning-runner angle is not by itself a differentiator.
founderCommand.prompt: FOUNDER ACTION (App Store Connect, not a Claude session). Read both drafts, pick or edit one line each, and paste into the Promotional Text field for both apps. No build, no resubmission, no review cycle. RunSmart: .agent-os/distribution/messaging.md (rs-aso-002-draft). Resumely: docs/app-store/en-metadata.md (anti-generic-AI variant). If you approve neither, say so in the file so it stops resurfacing as an open item.

founderCommand.title: #4 HOLD — WP-53 (guest path) stays CLOSED. One live event opens it, and it has not happened yet
founderCommand.body: WP-53 is conditional and this update does not open it. It opens on the first live sign_in_failed from 1.1.2 carrying has_underlying_error: false — a genuinely bare code 1000 the device cannot diagnose. If the property comes back true, read underlying_error_domain/_code, name the precondition (device not signed into iCloud vs Apple ID without 2FA vs genuine app fault) and fix with copy plus a check, which is days of work rather than a guest-architecture rebuild. THIRD OUTCOME, NAME IT NOW SO IT IS NOT MISREAD: code-1000 volume drops with has_underlying_error never going true. That means 1.1.2's iCloud-naming copy was the fix and WP-53 stays closed permanently. A quiet funnel is not 'no data'. Target read 2026-07-29 (T+7), but it is event-driven, not calendar-driven — one event decides it.
founderCommand.prompt: RunSmart iOS, run at T+7 (2026-07-29) or as soon as any sign_in_failed arrives from app_version 1.1.2, whichever is first. PostHog project 171597 (fingerprint it first). Pull every sign_in_failed on app_version 1.1.2 and report, per event: has_underlying_error, underlying_error_domain, underlying_error_code, underlying_error_description, device model, and $is_testflight/$is_sideloaded/$is_emulator. Then state which of the three branches fired — true (name the precondition), false (open WP-53), or zero failures with reduced volume (copy was the fix, WP-53 stays closed). Do NOT compute a rate; n will be tiny and a rate here would repeat the exact error that produced the refuted P0.

founderCommand.title: #5 BUILD, CHEAP — WP-55: turn on the observability you are already paying for, now, while the cohort is young
founderCommand.body: Error tracking is off in BOTH PostHog projects (zero $exception events) and session replay is off in RunSmart (zero $snapshot in 90 days) while 9 users rage-clicked the main screen 30 times. RunSmart's $screen_name also resolves to raw SwiftUI class names like UIHostingController<ModifiedContent<AnyView, RootModifier>>, which makes replay and rage-click data undiagnosable even after replay is switched on. A brand-new release with a fresh cohort is exactly the moment this is worth most — every day it stays off is a day of first-impression failures that leave no trace. Small packet, already scoped.
founderCommand.prompt: Execute work packet executive-os/work-packets/WP-55-observability-switches.md. Success signal: $snapshot events present in RunSmart, $exception events present in both projects, and $screen_name showing human-readable names like PlanView rather than raw SwiftUI generic class names. Sonnet 5 route.

founderCommand.title: #6 DO NOT — the explicit not-doing list, and the second trap
founderCommand.body: Do NOT open monetization or Gate A (EXD-009/EXD-013/EXD-018 all still hold — activation is not proven, it is finally just measurable). Do NOT spend on paid acquisition ($0 until organic activation is proven). Do NOT start Adaptive Coach Phase 2 (its gate needs 2 weeks live AND >=20 real adaptive_coach_shown users; neither condition is met). Do NOT touch Garmin (EXD-021 — parked on the עוסק מורשה registration, not on a date). Do NOT read the Resumely funnel before 2026-07-29, and do NOT compare any post-1.4.5 number against 12.5% or 10.0% — those are UNMEASURED, not low, and the comparison is meaningless rather than imprecise. THE SECOND TRAP: there are 45 open packets and 34 stranded items, and the 14-day wait for a cohort makes sweeping them feel productive. It is not — the 30d spend split is already 28.9% on product against a >=60% target ($1,175 product vs $2,884 meta). Waiting well means shipping the review prompt and WP-55, not reorganizing the backlog.

tracks.name: Portfolio Activation Playbook v2
tracks.standing: The canonical activation contract remains Intent -> Setup -> First value -> Verified value -> Repeat value. Founder-excluded mature D7 snapshots still drive decisions. Founder confirmed RunSmart 1.1.1 (25) live on 2026-07-21; Resumely 1.4.3 (13) remains live while the completed seven-story UI/copy pass is prepared as 1.4.4. WP-48 measured Resumely at 0 of the required 20 clean uploaders with a 4.7/week arrival rate, projecting maturity to ~2026-08-18. A 1.4.4 release resets the exact-version cohort clock; do not combine its cohort with 1.4.3.

Post-live gates reset on each new version. RunSmart 1.1.1 can now be segmented by app_version/app_build. Resumely's current 1.4.3 cohort projects to ~2026-08-18, but shipping 1.4.4 resets the exact-version clock and requires a fresh maturity date.

Live 1.1.1 (25), real App Store install (not TestFlight/sideloaded/emulator), iPhone17,1: 5 consecutive ASAuthorizationError 1000 at screen=sign_in_wall, 0 completions. tracks.name: Resumely FTUX upgrade

tracks.name: Resumely web export — observability fix
tracks.standing: The web export-observability fix merged in PR #114 on 2026-07-11. Core CI, GitGuardian, CodeRabbit and Vercel checks passed; the known Cloudflare Workers check failed independently of the Vercel production path. The export mechanism was already QA-verified end to end. The next valid read is after five real founder/QA-excluded web completers pass through the newly instrumented export path.

items.text: Optimization page now emits optimized_viewed, export_cta_seen, export_pdf_tapped; download route emits authenticated export_started then exactly one of export_success/export_failed, all tagged with optimization_id, platform=web, source, format, renderer/error code

Regression test failed 3/3 before the fix, passes 3/3 after; targeted + full ESLint clean (11 pre-existing warnings, 0 new errors); production build succeeds; TypeScript clean outside documented pre-existing test errors; smoke test generated a 7,328-byte PDF
PR #114 merged 2026-07-11; Vercel check passed and the instrumented export path is now available for measurement
Re-measure optimized_viewed → export_cta_seen → export_pdf_tapped → export_started → export_success/export_failed after 5 real web completers

The audit framing carries over; the Gemini delivery vehicle does not.

When she's on OpenAI: set her up on Codex and build Michal's OS; then file the first toolkit template so the pilot feeds the AI Audit Toolkit product.

artifacts.note: Durable artifacts built across Builder OS, Agentic OS and the product repos, so nothing gets lost. `state` is the honest storage truth, not a quality rating: `safe` = committed and pushed to a branch on origin; `branch-only` = pushed but never merged to main, so it is invisible to anyone reading main; `local-only` = exists on one disk and one `git clean` from gone. Verified against origin on 2026-07-15 — re-verify before trusting a link after any branch cleanup.

suggestedSessions.title: Ship the App Store review prompt as 1.4.6 (16)
suggestedSessions.why: Zero ratings on the primary product after two months live, and no prompt on the main success path — only a TODO comment. It is also the one build worth shipping during a 14-day measurement wait.
suggestedSessions.outcome: A gated review request on export success, an analytics event proving the ask rate, and 1.4.6 (16) staged at the founder archive boundary.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Add a StoreKit review request on export success in Resumely iOS and ship it as 1.4.6 (16). Trigger ONLY after a PDF is actually delivered — never on launch, never on optimization completion, never on a failure path. Gate to once per app version and require at least one completed export; persist the gate. Exclude is_internal_tester persons and Debug builds (WP-48 S2-B is open; internal traffic is 27% of persons). Use SKStoreReviewController / RequestReviewAction via the SwiftUI environment and never block UI — iOS may silently decline to display it. Emit an analytics event when the request is issued so the ask rate is measurable. Write red-first tests for the gate logic before implementing. Resolve or delete the dead '// TODO: SKStoreReviewController.requestReview()' at Ambassador/AmbassadorSuccessView.swift:37 so there is one code path. Run the full suite plus EN and HE launch smoke on iOS 26.5 (NOT 26.3.1 — it crashes the XCTest host). Do not touch the WP-51 milestone or the WP-50 denominator. Stop at the founder-controlled archive boundary.

suggestedSessions.title: WP-55 — turn on error tracking and session replay while the cohort is young
suggestedSessions.why: Error tracking is off in both PostHog projects and session replay is off in RunSmart, while 9 users rage-clicked the main screen 30 times. A fresh release is when this is worth most; every day it stays off is first-impression failure that leaves no trace.
suggestedSessions.outcome: $snapshot events in RunSmart, $exception events in both projects, and human-readable $screen_name values instead of raw SwiftUI generic class names.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Execute work packet executive-os/work-packets/WP-55-observability-switches.md. Success signal: $snapshot events present in RunSmart, $exception present in both projects, and $screen_name showing names like PlanView rather than UIHostingController<ModifiedContent<AnyView, RootModifier>>.

growth.note: WP-32 closed inconclusive on 2026-07-12. X/Twitter → activation & GTM research filed 2026-07-19: executive-os/research/2026-07-19-x-twitter-activation-gtm-hooks.md (native X login-walled; hooks from HU/ATS/AI-resume language + prior demand-mining). Next: founder picks 1 Resumely + 1 RunSmart ASO/first-screen line to draft; no publish without approval. WP-31 remains the next approved draft-only organic asset.

traffic.label: Resumely — unique guest_mode_started people per week (is_internal_tester excluded)
traffic.story: Refreshed 2026-07-21 from PostHog 270848. Series changed: the old resume_uploaded proxy is retired — WP-46 Story 10 removed its production call site in 1.4.3, so any series built on it now trends to zero for a code reason, not a traffic reason. Volume is now guest_mode_started (pre-auth arrival, WP-50-aligned), which is why the numbers jump versus the 2026-07-19 read: different, better denominator, not a traffic surge. Arrivals peaked at 26/wk (Jun 22) and have settled at 12-15/wk. Downstream in those same weeks: file_selected 0-5, optimized 0-4, exported 0-2. Arrival is not the problem; everything after it is.

traffic.label: RunSmart — unique Application Installed people per week (filterTestAccounts)
traffic.story: Refreshed 2026-07-21 from PostHog 171597. Install volume is steady at ~7-15/wk and essentially unchanged since June — 1.1.1 shipping did not move arrivals, nor should it have. Onboarding in those same weeks: 0-7 people/wk started, and plan_generated 0-6. The entry loss is the whole story, and the 2026-07-21 sign-in evidence now names its cause: SIWA fails with code 1000 on the live binary for Apple IDs that have not previously authorized RunSmart.

funnels.name: Resumely
funnels.tag: iOS · WP-50 contract · 30d through 2026-07-21 · SUPERSEDED BY 1.4.5 — diagnostic only, NOT a baseline
funnels.story: SUPERSEDED 2026-07-22. These numbers (12 file-selected -> 7 optimized -> 1 preview-rendered, with 3 exporters) are retained because they are the evidence that FOUND the defect, not because they measure anything. The funnel is non-monotonic — 3 exported while only 1 rendered a preview, impossible if the milestone fired — and WP-51 has now explained why: optimized_preview_rendered was gated on the separately-fetched sections array while the preview renders from optimization_id alone, so the milestone was suppressed with a real resume on screen. It shipped defective in its 1.4.1 form, so it NEVER fired reliably and there is NO valid pre-1.4.5 baseline. 12.5% and 10.0% are UNMEASURED, not low. 1.4.5 (15) went live 2026-07-22T15:23:44Z carrying the fix; its cohort is the first valid one. Next reads: 2026-07-29 monotonicity check (optimized_preview_rendered >= export_success — an instrument correctness test, NOT an activation figure), then 2026-08-05 definitive against the EXD-022 >=20 clean-activation gate. Read hygiene on both: exclude the founder person, is_internal_tester persons and QA/bot bursts (54 of 199 persons carried the flag and generated 49% of events), and fingerprint the project first — the MCP banner has claimed 171597 while serving 270848.

funnels.name: RunSmart
funnels.tag: iOS · sign-in wall, live 1.1.1 (25) only · 2026-07-20 → 07-21 (n=3, outage evidence) · sign-in P0 framing REFUTED 2026-07-22
funnels.story: UPDATED 2026-07-22. The wall failures in this funnel are real, but the conclusion drawn from them — that Sign in with Apple was broken for every new user — is refuted. A genuine revoke-and-retry on public 1.1.1 completed first-time consent with zero sign_in_failed. What survives: 10 code-1000 events across 3 real devices in July with 0 completions on any, and 0 non-founder signups since 2026-06-18, cause still unnamed. 1.1.2 (27) went live 2026-07-22T17:04:47Z carrying has_underlying_error, failure copy that names iCloud, and a double-tap guard. The next read is a property value on a single event, not a rate — computing a rate on this n is precisely the error that produced the refuted verdict. Live PostHog 171597 refresh 2026-07-21, filtered to app_version=1.1.1 / app_build=25 only. The WP-47/WP-51 instrumentation is confirmed working in production: named wall events, error_code, error_domain, screen and version/build attribution all arrive. What it shows is the wall failing. Of 3 people who saw it, 2 tapped, 1 completed and 1 failed — and the failure was 5 consecutive ASAuthorizationEr

rows.metric: BOTH APPS LIVE 2026-07-22 (store-verified, Apple lookup API)
rows.resumely: 1.4.5 (15) at 15:23:44Z — first build whose activation number means anything.
rows.runsmart: 1.1.2 (27) at 17:04:47Z — diagnosis build; one property decides WP-53.

rows.metric: Real D7 activation — VOID as a baseline, not a low number
rows.resumely: UNMEASURED. The optimized_preview_rendered milestone was defective from its 1.4.1 form and never fired reliably, so 0%, 12.5% and 10.0% are all artifacts of a broken instrument. Do not compare any post-1.4.5 figure against them.
rows.runsmart: UNMEASURED for a different reason: 0 organic run completions in the App Store era, so there is no denominator to activate against.

rows.metric: Live 30d funnel (2026-07-21) — SUPERSEDED, kept only as the evidence that exposed the defect
rows.resumely: 12 file-selected -> 7 optimized -> 1 preview-rendered -> 3 exported. The impossibility (more exports than previews) is now EXPLAINED and FIXED by WP-51 in 1.4.5. Retained as the diagnostic that found the bug, NOT as a baseline. Next valid read: 2026-07-29 monotonicity check, 2026-08-05 definitive.
rows.runsmart: 3 saw the sign-in wall -> 2 tapped -> 1 in, 1 hard-failed 5x with code 1000. The failures are real; the 'broken for everyone' reading drawn from them is refuted.

rows.resumely: 145 reached → 20 tapped upload → 16 uploaded → 8 job → 2 optimized → 1 exported
rows.runsmart: 23 organic App Store users → 1 onboarded → 1 planned → 0 ran

rows.resumely: arrival → file selected: ~12-15 guests/wk arrive, 0-5 ever select a résumé. The cliff moved earlier than the old upload→optimization framing suggested
rows.runsmart: install → onboarding, and the cause is now named: the sign-in wall is not a persuasion step, it is a broken gate that returns Apple error 1000 to first-time users

rows.metric: Sign-in integrity (EXD-023 REFUTED by its own step 2, 2026-07-22)
rows.resumely: Not applicable — guest path reaches optimization with no account. That architectural difference, not instrumentation quality, is why this funnel is readable.
rows.runsmart: NOT broken for everyone. Revoke-and-retry on public 1.1.1 completed a genuine first-time consent flow: sign_in_completed 10:34:21Z, ZERO sign_in_failed, new auth.identities row 10:34:20Z. Withdrawn: 'has not worked since 2026-06-18'. Still unexplained: 10 code-1000 events on 3 real devices (not 4 — iPhone99,7 is not an Apple model) with 0 completions, and 0 non-founder signups since 2026-06-18. 1.1.2 is live and instrumented; has_underlying_error decides WP-53 on one event.

rows.metric: Audit cross-check (2026-07-21) — why the PostHog AI number is 2.7x optimistic
rows.resumely: A PostHog-generated audit reported 12.8% activation (25/196). It excluded 4 email-matched persons; 54 of 199 persons (27%) carry is_internal_tester and generate 49% of all events. Clean equivalent is ~4.8% (7 optimization_completed / ~145 clean actives). The audit also invented its own activation definition, unaware the WP-50 contract lives in the repo rather than in PostHog
rows.runsmart: Same audit claimed sign_in_failed carries no diagnostics ('error and provider are null') and made fixing it a top-5 weekly priority. It queried property names that do not exist — the real ones (error_code=1000, error_domain, screen=sign_in_wall) are populated on all 12 events. Instrumentation is fine; the Apple config is not

rows.resumely: save_failed 21 events / 20 people, optimization_apply_failed 12/9, optimization_state_recovery_failed 24/12 — ALL with zero diagnostic properties (0 of 57 rows populated). Users who do activate then hit a blind wall
rows.runsmart: plan_generation ~29% failure rate (28 started → 12 succeeded, 8 failed). coach_message_sent silent for 30d while coach_thread_opened still fires — likely silent breakage

rows.resumely: 92% one-and-done (217/234 persons active in exactly one week over 60d); W2 return ~6%. WAU grew 6→63 over 7 weeks then fell to 34 — growth is almost entirely new users, not returning ones
rows.runsmart: D1 10.7% (9/84), D7/D30 not computable — 69 of 84 person IDs are anonymous installs that never authenticated, so the denominator is unusable

rows.resumely: Activation milestone optimized_preview_rendered fires for only 3 people in 60d while more people export than preview — funnel is non-monotonic, so the milestone is suspect
rows.runsmart: app_version/app_build now land correctly on the live build. Note: marketing_version is NOT in this project's taxonomy — plans referencing it should read $app_version / app_build

rows.resumely: 1.4.5 (15) LIVE, released 2026-07-22T15:23:44Z (id6776752349). Carries WP-51 + WP-52 on top of 1.4.4's UI/copy pass.
rows.runsmart: 1.1.2 (27) LIVE, released 2026-07-22T17:04:47Z (id6768297840). Diagnosis build: has_underlying_error, iCloud-naming failure copy, SIWA double-tap guard.

rows.metric: Target (EXD-022, and the first measurable cohort starts NOW)
rows.resumely: >=20 clean activations on a WORKING milestone, no calendar deadline. The 1.4.5 cohort clock started 2026-07-22T15:23:44Z and is the first valid baseline in the product's history. 2026-08-01 = reporting checkpoint, no verdict authority. 2026-09-01 = strategy-revisit trigger.
rows.runsmart: Secondary (EXD-015). No activation target while the front door still fails for an unknown fraction. Gate is the has_underlying_error read, not a rate.

activationHeadline.note: REFRESH 2026-07-21 (live PostHog, projects 171597 + 270848). Three things changed and they are not equally good news. (1) RunSmart's new instrumentation works and immediately caught the sign-in P0 reproducing on the LIVE 1.1.1 public binary — this closes the 'is it outage or persuasion' question in favour of outage, and it invalidates reading any RunSmart FTUX number as a product signal until sign-in is fixed. (2) Resumely's canonical WP-50 funnel is readable but n=12/30d and internally inconsistent (more exporters than preview-renderers), so the activation milestone optimized_preview_rendered is itself suspect and must be fixed before the 2026-08-01 EXD-015 target review means anything. (3) Store-verified release truth: RunSmart 1.1.1 live 2026-07-20T20:38:19Z, Resumely still 1.4.3 — though 1.4.4 events are already in PostHog, so a non-App-Store build is circulating. Prior clean-funnel row retained below for continuity. Clean-funnel row added 2026-07-19 from a founder/QA/bot-excluded PostHog read (projects 171597 + 270848, 90d) filed as executive-os/ACTIVATION-AUTOPSY-2026-07-19.md and per-repo docs/plans/2026-07-19-activation-cliff-fix-plan.md. Exclusions: founder person, +fable-qa*/+export-wall* aliases, is_internal_tester (45 persons / 1,216 events in Resumely), burst chains >=3 events/sec, and RunSmart behavioural founder/QA heuristics (>=6 sessions or >=3 app versions). Mature founder-excluded D7 from 2026-07-12 remains the decision contract; the clean-funnel row is diagnostic, not a replacement cohort. Correction: free_ats_completed DOES carry score_bucket — live 90d is 5x '0-40' + 5x '41-60', so every real free-ATS user saw <=60.

posthogDashboards.decisionSnapshot: P0 OUTAGE CONFIRMED 2026-07-21 on live 1.1.1: 5/5 Apple code-1000 failures at sign_in_wall on a real App Store install. Activation numbers downstream of the wall are not interpretable until this is fixed. Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0% (0/13 through 2026-07-12)
posthogDashboards.exclusions: Sign-in read is unfiltered by design — every code-1000 event matters regardless of who hit it; install-channel flags ($is_testflight/$is_sideloaded/$is_emulator) were checked per-event instead
posthogDashboards.cohortCutoff: Mature D7 through 2026-07-12 · live 1.1.1 sign-in read 2026-07-21
posthogDashboards.warning: The linked operational dashboard uses rolling 30-day charts, independent event totals and broader traffic. It can include founder signals and is not the mature ordered D7 cohort.

posthogDashboards.decisionSnapshot: Live WP-50 contract 2026-07-21: 12 file-selected → 7 optimized → 1 preview-rendered, 3 exported (30d, is_internal_tester excluded). Not decision-grade: n=12 cannot test the 20% target, and the funnel is non-monotonic. Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0% (0/73 through 2026-07-12)
posthogDashboards.exclusions: is_internal_tester persons excluded at the person level (any tagged event disqualifies the person for the window)
posthogDashboards.cohortCutoff: Mature D7 through 2026-07-12 · WP-50 contract refresh 2026-07-21
posthogDashboards.warning: Two retired events will silently produce false zeros: resume_uploaded lost its production call site in 1.4.3 (WP-46 Story 10), and resume_upload_succeeded sits behind the sign-in guard so it can never exceed sign_in_completed (WP-48 Defect B). The canonical denominator is resume_file_selected. Separately, optimized_preview_rendered — the activation milestone itself — appears to under-fire and is the next measurement fix.

clocks.what: BOTH APPS LIVE (store-verified): Resumely 1.4.5 (15) at 15:23:44Z, RunSmart 1.1.2 (27) at 17:04:47Z. The EXD-022 activation counter can finally move off zero — the 1.4.5 cohort is the first ever measured on a working milestone.

Sign-in P0 REFUTED by revoke-and-retry on public 1.1.1: genuine first-time consent completed, zero sign_in_failed. EXD-023's regression framing withdrawn and corrected in place. Still unexplained: 3 real devices, 10 code-1000 events, 0 non-founder signups since 06-18.

Agentic OS parser bug found and fixed: keyed_fields skipped bold '**Current Phase:**' blocks, so the dashboard had been reporting RunSmart as '1.0.9 awaiting review' while 1.1.2 was live. 96 tests pass, 2 added.
T+1 reachability check: do non-founder events arrive carrying app_version 1.1.2 and 1.4.5? Binary check, ~10 minutes. Gates every later read.

T+7 double read: RunSmart's first live sign_in_failed with has_underlying_error (decides WP-53 on one event), and Resumely's funnel monotonicity check (optimized_preview_rendered >= export_success). The Resumely one validates the INSTRUMENT, not activation.
EXD-022 mandatory reporting checkpoint. Produces a written progress read with an honest clean-activation count, including 'too early'. NO verdict authority — this is deliberately not a decision date.
T+14 definitive 1.4.5 cohort read against the >=20 clean-activation gate. First trustworthy activation number in the product's history.
EXD-022 separate trigger: if nothing is measurable by here, revisit the activation STRATEGY rather than the metric.

kk: SIGN-IN, CORRECTED 2026-07-22 — supersedes every earlier sign-in entry in this list: first-time Sign in with Apple WORKS. A genuine revoke (Stop Using Apple ID) + delete + reinstall of public 1.1.1 (25) produced sign_in_wall_viewed 10:33:57Z -> sign_in_completed 10:34:21Z with ZERO sign_in_failed, a new auth.identities row at 10:34:20Z, then full onboarding, HealthKit sync and plan generation. The earlier 'broken for every new user' verdict rested on two errors that both generalize: a zero-success window at ~26 launchers/month cannot distinguish universal from partial failure (the p~0.007 figure assumed the uniformity it was meant to test), and Apple's sub is stable across revoke/re-authorize so a new identity row proves 'new to Supabase', never 'new to Apple'. Also iPhone99,7 is not a real Apple model, so 3 real devices failed, not 4. WHAT SURVIVES: 10 code-1000 events on those 3 devices in July with 0 completions, and 0 non-founder signups since 2026-06-18. Leading untested hypothesis: device/account preconditions (no iCloud session, or Apple ID without 2FA) — both surface as a bare code 1000 and the shipped copy named neither until 1.1.2.

RATINGS, NEW 2026-07-22: both apps have ZERO App Store ratings (averageUserRating 0, userRatingCount 0, Apple lookup API). Resumely — primary, ~55 users/7d, live since June — has NO review prompt on the main success path; the only requestReview in the codebase is a '// TODO:' at Ambassador/AmbassadorSuccessView.swift:37. RunSmart's prompt exists but fires from requestAfterFirstRunIfNeeded() and zero organic users have ever completed a run, so it has effectively never fired. This is the largest unforced loss currently visible in the portfolio.

MEASUREMENT PLUMBING, NEW 2026-07-22: the Agentic OS parser had been silently reporting stale status. keyed_fields skipped any line starting with '*', so the bold '**Current Phase:**' blocks every session writes were invisible and the dashboard fell through to an older plain block in the same file — showing RunSmart as '1.0.9 (23) submitted, awaiting App Review' on the day 1.1.2 (27) went live. Freshness read 'Fresh' and confidence 'High' throughout, because the last-commit date was genuinely current. Fixed at the parser; 96 tests pass, 2 added. Lesson: agreeing trust signals are not independent trust signals.

SIGN-IN (2026-07-19, hardened): RunSmart has 7 non-cancel ASAuthorizationError code-1000 failures across 3 distinct fresh installs — iPhone99,7 and iPhone12,1 on public 1.0.9 (2026-07-15) and iPhone17,2 on build 24 (2026-07-19) — with 0 sign_in_completed on any of them. The single sign_in_completed on 1.0.9 (2026-07-16) is the founder's already-provisioned iPhone 13 (iPhone14,5). Supabase auth logs covering today's 11:03-11:04 UTC failures contain no /token request, so the failure occurs on-device before Supabase is contacted; Supabase provider_disabled is ruled out for that window (older 07-15 failures are outside the 24h log retention and cannot be checked). Code 1000 is also produced by a device with no iCloud account and by locally-installed builds whose provisioning lacks the SIWA capability — build 24 was not an App Store install, so that data point is weak. Verdict: strong unresolved P0 signal, still NOT a proven production outage. One clean repro on an iCloud-signed device with the public binary settles it.

SIGN-IN P0 CONFIRMED IN PRODUCTION (2026-07-21, supersedes the 07-19 'not a proven outage' verdict): on the LIVE 1.1.1 (25) App Store binary, one person on iPhone17,1 produced 5 consecutive ASAuthorizationError 1000 events at screen=sign_in_wall between 12:22 and 12:25 UTC on 2026-07-20, with 0 sign_in_completed. $is_testflight=false, $is_sideloaded=false, $is_emulator=false, so the weak-provisioning explanation that softened the build-24 data point does not apply here. Cumulative 12 code-1000 events across 4 distinct devices on 3 builds, and every sign_in_completed on record belongs to an Apple ID that had already authorized RunSmart. The question is no longer whether this is real; it is which link in the Apple capability chain is broken.

RUNSMART 1.1.1 MEASUREMENT (2026-07-21, VERIFIED IN PRODUCTION): app_version/app_build attribution, named sign-in-wall events, error_code, error_domain and screen all arrive correctly from the public build — WP-47/WP-51 worked. Caveat for anyone writing queries: the property is $app_version / app_build. marketing_version is NOT in project 171597's taxonomy, and several plan documents reference it; a query using that name returns null for every row and looks like missing data rather than a wrong property name.

CLEAN FUNNELS (2026-07-19, founder/QA/bot-excluded, 90d): RunSmart 23 organic App Store users → 1 onboarding_started → 1 plan_generated → 0 run_started (95.7% lost at the first screen). Resumely 145 reached → 20 tapped upload → 16 uploaded → 8 job_added → 2 optimization_started → 1 export_success (87.5% of uploaders never optimize).

CONTAMINATION SCALE: Resumely raw dashboards are ~90% non-organic — 45 is_internal_tester persons (1,216 events) plus automated QA chains firing 3-9 events/sec (latest run 2026-07-18) generate nearly all raw fit_check_*, save_failed and optimization_apply_* volume. RunSmart has no is_internal_tester flag at all; exclusions there rely on behavioural heuristics (>=6 sessions or >=3 app versions) plus 39 simulator persons on model x86_64.

CORRECTION: free_ats_completed DOES carry score_bucket (an earlier read queried the wrong property names and wrongly reported it null). Live 90d: 5 events '0-40' and 5 events '41-60' — every real free-ATS user saw a score of 60 or below, half saw 40 or below.

BUILD CONTENT: RunSmart 1.1.1 (25) is founder-confirmed LIVE and carries the activation measurement fixes. Resumely 1.4.3 (13) remains live; the complete seven-story UI/copy pass is branch-only and targeted for 1.4.4, with no version bump or ASC action yet.
ASC 2026-07-21: RunSmart 1.1.1 (25) LIVE, founder-confirmed. Resumely 1.4.3 (13) remains the public build while 1.4.4 is prepared.

PostHog refresh 2026-07-19: RunSmart directional ordered funnel 63→6→3→0→0 (Jun 1–Jul 19); Resumely directional ordered funnel 14→4→2 upload→optimize→export (last 30d). Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0/13 and 0/73 from 2026-07-12.
Resumely PR #97 reconciled Release A Stories 1-6 and the exact 1.4.2 (12) version stamp onto origin/main; 151 tests passed, 1 intentional skip, 0 failures, so main can rebuild the active binary.

Resumely 1.4.2 production readiness is No: 82 exact-version rows across 5 raw people, 4 excluded and 0 clean post-release entrants. Mature D7 is 0/0 undefined; tagging is complete and the live property audit found no prohibited content.

RunSmart 1.0.9 post-live snapshot at 2026-07-16 05:30 UTC found 2 clean physical installs, 0 mature entrants and 5 non-cancel sign-in failures across both people with 0 sign-in completions. This is a P0 signal, not a proven outage at n=2.
Both remain at 0% real D7 activation on the 2026-07-12 mature read: Resumely 0/73; RunSmart 0/13.

Resumely D7 wall = first seen→upload→optimization: 73 first seen, 9 uploaded, 0 optimized, 0 exported. PR #114 web export observability is merged; wait for 5 real web completers before reading that new path.
RunSmart wall = install→onboarding and plan→run: 13 mature installs, 1 onboarded/planned, 0 ran. WP-40 S1+S2 merged via PR #84; WP-42 has no clean production disclosure cohort yet.
Traffic collapsed post-launch-push; WP-29 web funnel fixed + verified.
One backend tester account owned 22/23 of all Resumely Supabase optimizations pre-hardening — analytics identity/alias wiring shipped 07-07 (Story 1) to fix this going forward.

Garmin: brand attribution + Internal-Test-vs-Commercial tiering are well-grounded in Garmin's public docs (matches 3 real rejections). Fee structure for RunSmart specifically and the Gate 1-4 checklist are founder-confirmed directly with Marc via email/attachments (2026-07-08) — the 07-06 research pass's "unconfirmed" flag was based on Garmin's generic public docs, not RunSmart's actual terms with Marc; no further written confirmation needed.
ku: Why does ASAuthorizationError 1000 occur on the live binary — is it a missing Sign In with Apple entitlement on the App Store distribution profile, a Services ID / key misconfiguration in the Apple Developer portal, or a bundle-ID mismatch in the Supabase Apple provider? All three produce code 1000 on-device before Supabase is contacted, which matches the observed auth-log silence.

Would shipping a guest path (reach plan generation with no account, as Resumely does) be faster and more valuable than fixing Apple sign-in? It removes the outage from the critical path permanently rather than repairing one gate.

Why does optimized_preview_rendered fire for only 3 people in 60 days when more people reach export? Is the WKWebView navigation callback unreliable, or is the event gated on a condition that rarely holds?
Which build is emitting Resumely 1.4.4 events while the App Store still serves 1.4.3 — TestFlight, a local Debug install, or an unrecorded submission?
Can the next Hebrew distribution test produce a measurable answer after WP-32 closed without attribution? It must use unique campaign links and verified analytics access before publishing.
Did Resumely Stories 2-4 (PR #90, merged 07-08) actually move the upload-picker friction numbers for real (non-founder) traffic? Re-read once enough post-merge volume exists.
Does the merged web export-observability path reveal real web export failures after 5 real completers, or does it confirm the button works for organic users?
Is Resumely's cumulative clean export count 0 or 1? The saved clean July 6 report says 0 while a later manual dashboard claim says 1; resolve with a reproducible query before citing it.
Voice-coach flag: DECIDED 2026-07-09 — deferred (stays off), review at the 07-12 activation re-read or after WP-40 lands. No longer an open question.
uk: Michal Track C fully drafted — blocked only on her live Gemini account.

WP files lag Codex/Cursor sessions — dashboards lie until packets are updated. Three Codex/Cursor sessions found work sitting uncommitted or on detached HEAD this week (ResumeBuilder iOS APP x2, RunSmart Web) 07-06/07/08 — branched, verified, and preserved each time (PR #89, #90, #116), but a recurring risk worth remembering before trusting 'no local changes.' WP-32/33/34 were the exception this round — Cursor updated and pushed the packets itself (commit fe9ad95).

status.json's needsDecision still lists "RunSmart iOS: if build 8 is rejected again" — stale, from before 1.0.7(21) shipped and passed review. Not a portfolio-hq bug; RunSmart iOS's own task files need a refresh to clear it. Same staleness pattern flagged by ./agentic-os verify for COO-LATEST-REVIEW.md (references build 18, current is build 21).
ResumeBuilder IOS APP is missing tasks/ERRORS.md and docs/agent-os/project-context.md — flagged as a required-read gap by two separate Codex sessions now.

Artifacts strand silently. Both FTUX audits — the evidence base for all 13 Resumely stories and all three RunSmart work packets — sit on branches never merged to main, and the Resumely one was untracked in every branch until 2026-07-15 (rescued via PR #96). The Artifacts tab now lists each one with its real storage state; treat `branch-only` as one branch-cleanup away from invisible.
RunSmart E1 is deliberately deferred until sign-in works and at least 10 clean mature-D7 entrants exist; assignment instrumentation is not active work.
RunSmart iOS has ~30 untracked Finder-duplicate '* 2.swift'/'* 2.md' files cluttering the repo — pre-existing, same issue already named in status.json's blockers as blocking clean archives.
uu: Does the PostHog leak run both ways (Resumely → RunSmart project)?
Do other dashboards carry founder/QA contamination (the 50% inflation pattern)?
Is 'activation' the right north star before there's traffic to activate?

weekPriorities.title: Resumely — ship the review prompt (1.4.6). Zero ratings is the biggest unforced loss in the portfolio
weekPriorities.body: Primary product, ~55 users/7d, live since June, zero App Store ratings, and no review prompt on the main success path — the only requestReview in the codebase is a TODO comment. This is the one piece of product work that compounds during the 14 days you are otherwise waiting for the 1.4.5 cohort to fill. Trigger on export success only, gate once per version, exclude internal testers and Debug.

weekPriorities.title: Both apps — run the T+1 reachability check tomorrow, then leave the cohorts alone until 07-29
weekPriorities.body: Confirm events arrive carrying app_version 1.1.2 and 1.4.5. That is the whole check. After it passes, the correct action for 6 days is to not read the funnel: the 1.4.5 cohort is the first valid activation baseline in the product's history and reading it early produces a number that looks like a finding and is not one.

weekPriorities.title: RunSmart — the sign-in thread is NOT closed, it is now event-gated on one property
weekPriorities.body: The P0 is refuted but 0 non-founder signups since 2026-06-18 is unchanged and 3 real devices still fail with no explanation. 1.1.2 is live and instrumented. WP-53 opens on has_underlying_error: false and on nothing else. Do not build the guest path in advance and do not declare sign-in healthy.

weekPriorities.title: Free wins available with no build: ASO promo text on both listings, and WP-55 observability
weekPriorities.body: Promotional text changes on App Store Connect need no build and no review cycle, and both drafts have been written since 2026-07-11. WP-55 switches on error tracking in both PostHog projects and session replay in RunSmart — worth most right now, while a fresh cohort's first impressions are still happening.

weekPriorities.title: Do not spend the wait on the backlog — the spend split already says the ratio is wrong
weekPriorities.body: 45 open packets and 34 stranded items make sweeping feel productive during a measurement wait. 30d spend is 28.9% product vs a >=60% target ($1,175 product vs $2,884 meta). Ship the review prompt and WP-55; leave monetization, paid acquisition, Adaptive Coach Phase 2 and Garmin closed.
